text:
Under the vast canopy of the night sky, beneath twinkling stars, a small, sleepy town named Serenity slept soundly. Each house had its unique charm—a cottage with a thatched roof echoing laughter, a Victorian mansion with a rose-red façade, and an old-fashioned bank where a golden hour clock chimed each evening.

One quiet evening, the town's most unusual resident, a mysterious figure known only as "The Traveler," shuffled into the town's general store. He held a bundle wrapped in old cloth. The townsfolk exchanged glances; some knew him as a seeker of secrets, others just as a man without a past.

The Traveler purchased a single pebble, shuffling back out into the night with its oddity tucked under his coat.

Later that night, a strange vibration shook the cobblestones. Farmers looked at each other; something was afoot—or rather, underground.

The next day, news spread like wildfire. The Traveler was back—this
